Name: rate_distortion_neural_compression
Title: Information-Theoretic Neural Network Compression: A Rate-Distortion Approach
Short Hypothesis: Neural networks can be compressed using rate-distortion theory, which provides a fundamental limit on the trade-off between compression rate and distortion. We hypothesize that by optimizing the compression scheme to achieve this limit, we can develop more efficient and effective neural network compression methods.
Related Work: Recent works such as Spectral Quantization Routing, Training-Free Geometric Interference Cancellation, and Geometric Signal Separation have explored the use of geometric and information-theoretic methods for neural network compression and optimization. However, these works do not explicitly address the fundamental limits of compression and distortion. Our proposal fills this gap by providing a rate-distortion framework for neural network compression, which can be used to evaluate and improve existing methods.
Abstract: Neural network compression is a crucial technique for deploying deep learning models in resource-constrained environments. However, existing methods often rely on heuristics or empirical approaches, lacking a clear understanding of the fundamental limits of compression and distortion. In this work, we propose a rate-distortion approach to neural network compression, which provides a mathematical framework for understanding the trade-off between compression rate and distortion. We develop a novel compression scheme that optimizes the rate-distortion function, achieving a fundamental limit on the compression rate and distortion. Our approach provides a transparent and reproducible method for neural network compression, enabling the development of more efficient and effective compression methods.
Experiments: Evaluate the rate-distortion performance of our compression scheme on benchmark datasets and compare it to existing methods.
Investigate the effect of different distortion measures and compression rates on the performance of the compressed model.
Develop a theoretical analysis of the rate-distortion limit for neural networks and compare it to the empirical results.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The development of a rate-distortion framework for neural network compression may require significant mathematical and computational resources.
The compression scheme may not be compatible with all types of neural networks or applications.
The evaluation of the compression scheme may require large-scale experiments and simulations.
